entry.title: Re: Using EDI Envelopes in Integration Advisor
entry.content:
Hello Steve ( @Benko ),

If you export Runtime Artifacts for EDI Type Systems (like X12 or UN/EDIFACT), you will get 2 XSD files for two purposes.

The so-called MIG XSD (named <Your_MIG>_RD.xsd) describes the very specific message structure of your MIG with all the customizations you have done. This XSD allows you to perform a very detailed semantic validation if any payload corresponds to your MIG definition. Please check this XSD – this should include the Envelope segments.

Additionally, there is the so-called Message XSD (named like ASC-X12_850_004010.xsd). This XSD file is designed to be consumed in EDI Flow Steps (like EDI Splitter and EDI-To-XML Converter). These EDI Flow Steps need the Message XSD as input (and not the full Interchange XSD). This is the reason why the Envelope segments are not part of this XSD (by design!).

Feel free to have a look at this help page for more details how Integration Advisor artifacts are consumed: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/consuming-integration-advisor-artifacts-in-integration-flows .

I hope this answers your question.

Best regards,

Jörg

entry.title: Your opportunity to Influence SAP – Customer Engagement Initiative Projects 2025-2 for SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
An exciting opportunity to influence SAP has started with the first cycle of the 2025 SAP Customer Engagement Initiative (CEI). The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative enables you as an SAP customer or partner to get early insights into SAP’s product developments and directly work with the developers to define and shape future product directions.

We are offering several interesting opportunities for customers to influence SAP’s S/4HANA solutions.

Find here the list of all projects that are relevant for the SAP S4/HANA Community or browse the whole project list here.

The projects will be open for customer registration until July 25, 2025.


SAP S4/HANA and SAP S4/HANA Cloud projects for registration:

 

ERP Cloud Delivery & Excellence

Potential Workforce Person Data Correction Tool

Test Data Variants–Driven Test Process Flow Control and Execution

Tool-Agnostic Test Automation from AI-Generated Process Variants for ERP Systems

 

ERP Digital Supply Chain Cloud

Empowering Reliability: Data-Driven Maintenance Efficiency Analysis for Strategy Optimization

Freight Rate Management for transportation processes with a low to medium complexity

Reimagine Warehouse Management with Joule

 

ERP Finance & Risk

AI assisted creation of balance validation rules AND comment proposal

Financial Reporting according to IFRS18 in S4HANA Cloud

Financial Statements in the New Review Booklets Analytical Application

 

ERP Private Cloud

Best Practice for Inspection Checklists in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud

 

ERP Public Cloud

Actual Liquidity Analysis

AI Agent for Open Item Clearing

AI-powered chatbot (Joule) for Intelligent Margin Analysis

Assess and improve your Quote-to-Cash process with meaningful Business Health KPIs

Enhancing Payment Run Experience

Optimizing Partner Solution Support in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Ecosystem

Price Recommendation in S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition

Research: Payment Advice In Existing Business Process

Sales Contract Management - Artificial Intelligent Handling for Quantity/Value Consumption by Joule

SAP Business Suite: Agility and Innovation through seamless Integration and Harmonization

Successor for Create and Process Non-Assigned Handling Units (HU02)

 

ERP User Experience

AI-assisted error resolution and data entry for SAP Fiori apps

AI-assisted Smart Visualization of MyHome for To-Dos (situations)

 

How do I register on the Customer Influence website? A short overview about how to find and register for a CEI project can be found on influence.sap.com.

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

Thank you in advance for your participation!

entry.title: SAP Community Team Spotlight: Moshe Naveh - June 2025 Edition
entry.content:
You might have seen our iterations of recognizing active authors (one / two / three / four / five / six)  in SAP Community. We also want to introduce the team behind the SAP Community platform. This month we want to introduce @moshenaveh to you which I interviewed recently. Read more about how he got started and what his day to day looks like behind the scenes of the SAP Community.

 

Interview with Moshe

Santos, Marco (MS): How did you first get involved with the SAP Community? What was your starting point?

Moshe Naveh: I started back in 2009 when I was hired to be part of the Community team, back then it was still called SCN. My main responsibility at the time was supporting wiki users and editors. That was my first major project, and it gave me a great opportunity to get to know the community deeply. I also supported various operational and engagement-related tasks, so from the beginning, I was fully immersed in the day-to-day of the community and its contributors.

MS: That sounds great, now that we talked about the past let’s switch to the present: can you tell us what your current role on the SAP Community team is, and what a typical day looks like for you?

Moshe Naveh: I’m part of the SAP Community team, and my role is very diverse. A big part of my work is supporting community members across multiple channels. Whether it’s through the Community inbox, internal systems, or online discussions. I help identify different types of needs, like content, training, or even platform-related issues, and I work to address those in a way that benefits our users and stakeholders. 

My day usually starts with checking in on those support channels and then moves into daily moderation tasks. I also meet regularly with stakeholders and SAP Community collaborators, helping them understand how the community can support their goals. That includes coaching them on features, best practices, helping topic page editors with their contributions and how to increase engagement in the SAP Community.

I’m a people person, and I think that’s why I joined this team in the first place. It’s all about engagement and collaboration. On top of that, I’m constantly thinking of ways to improve the experience on the SAP Community and make it more enjoyable, whether that’s through challenges, engaging threads, or other interactive initiatives. One of the proudest moments for me was launching the "Blog It Forward" initiative.

Here are some examples:

What is your favorite dish or snack?

My favorite place is ... because...

Food for thought: What’s your favorite holiday/celebration dish?

 MS: That sounds great. What’s one part of the whole community experience that you're especially proud of contributing to?

Moshe Naveh: It’s hard to choose just one thing, but what really stands out to me is the day-to-day support I provide to community members and encourage them to engage. A great example is a very interesting blog series by SAP Sapphire speakers. I genuinely enjoy interacting with them, whether it’s through the community inbox or other channels and listening to their needs. Helping them overcome challenges they face and seeing their positive feedback afterward really warms my heart. Those moments of connection and appreciation are what keep me going and remind me why this work matters so much.

MS: You have mentioned before that you have a very memorable moment or time that you remember very fondly in the community. Do you want to elaborate a little bit on that?

Moshe Naveh: Yes, absolutely. Back in 2012, I was out for a jog. Funny enough, that’s often when my creativity kicks in. I was thinking about new ways to engage people in the community, and that’s when the idea for Blog It Forward came to me. It was a chain of blog posts where one person would write about themselves using a set of suggested questions, include some personal photos, and then “forward” the challenge to others. It quickly grew into a global storytelling movement with hundreds of participants. Even today, people remember it as one of the most enjoyable initiatives we’ve had and I’m incredibly proud to have sparked that.

MS: Can you elaborate a little bit on how you stay in touch with community needs and feedback?

Moshe Naveh: Sure. There are multiple ways we stay connected with the community. We regularly monitor our discussion forums and content areas to see what’s being posted. We also hold stakeholder meetings and participate in community calls where people share their thoughts directly. Many members reach out to us via email or internal channels, but the best way to contact the team is through the community@sap.com inbox. That’s where we gather a lot of insights and feedback. It helps us understand what members need and how we can support them better.

MS: If there’s one thing that you would really love to see more of in the community, what would that be?

Moshe Naveh: I always enjoy seeing and meeting new people in the community. Every new member brings their own qualities, experiences, and contributions, and that enriches the entire community. So, I’d love to see more new members joining. Creating a welcoming and inclusive environment is really close to my heart—that’s something we focused on in the Blog It Forward initiative and in other engaging threads. It’s also why I recently shared a blog post on using alternative text to make community content more accessible and inclusive.

MS: Great to hear that. And now we're at the last question of the interview: is there anything else that you would like to share?

Moshe Naveh: I think it's often a challenge for people to take that first step and start engaging in the community. But once they do, everything becomes easier. So I encourage everyone to just say hi, explore the different ways of participating, share your knowledge, and grow together with others. It’s truly a journey we can all take together—and it’s one that’s definitely worth it. 

MS: This is a  great message to end the interview. Thank you so much for joining today and sharing your experiences with the community!

entry.title: SAP Job Scheduling Service: Migrate jobs & schedules from one instance to another
entry.content:
SAP Job Scheduling Service: Migrate jobs & schedules from one instance to another

Do you need to migrate jobs and schedules from one service instance to another — for example, from a development environment to production? Most probably yes.

Is there automatic way to do that? Currently - no ☹️, but we can do it using the existing REST API endpoints of the Job Scheduling service.

In this post, we’ll walk through a Node.js script that automates this migration process for HTTP Endpoint jobs. This is especially useful when you want to replicate your job configurations across environments without manually recreating them.

⚠️ Notes: 

  • This is NOT a migration tool - it is just an example how to use existing REST APIs to migrate jobs and schedules
  • ✅This script only supports HTTP Endpoint jobs. 
  • ❌It does not support CF Tasks. (CF Tasks cannot be managed with the REST API)
  • 🔁It assumes that application routes/endpoints remain unchanged between environments.
  • 🖥️ It assumes that applications are configured to work with the new Job Scheduling service instance (applications are already migrated)

📦Prerequisites

Before you begin, make sure you have:

  • Two SAP Job Scheduler service instances (source and target)
  • Service keys for both instances saved as export-service-key.txt and import-service-key.txt from the BTP Cockpit
  • Node.js and npm installed
  • Basic familiarity with JavaScript and SAP BTP

🛠️ What the Script Does

The script performs the following steps:

  1. Authenticates with both the source and target Job Scheduling service instances using OAuth2.
  2. Retrieves all HTTP Endpoint jobs from the source instance.
  3. For each job, fetches its full definition including schedules.
  4. Cleans the job data by removing system-assigned fields like jobId and scheduleId.
  5. Recreates the job and its schedules in the target instance.

Creating service-keys

We need 2 service keys for the two instances so that we can "talk" to them. We can create these service keys either in the cockpit or using the CLI.

We can name them export-service-key for the source instance and import-service-key for the service instance where we want to "import" the data, the target.

You need to download them and place them in the same folder as the script.

 

💻The Code

You should have the following structure:

job-scheduling-service-rest-migrator/
├── index.js                 # Main script using ES module syntax
├── package.json             # Project metadata and dependencies
├── export-service-key.txt   # Service key for source service instance
├── import-service-key.txt   # Service key for target service instance
 

package.json

{
  "name": "job-scheduling-service-rest-migrator",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "Used to migrate jobs and schedules from one instance to another using the REST API",
  "main": "index.js",
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "start": "node index.js"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "axios": "^1.9.0",
    "qs": "^6.14.0"
  }
}
 

index.js

import fs from 'node:fs';
import axios from 'axios';
import qs from 'qs';


// 🔐 Load Service Keys
// 🔑 Get OAuth Tokens for the two service instances
// 📥 Fetch All Jobs from Source (Export) Instance
// 🔍 Fetch Job Details with Schedules
// 🧹 Clean Job Data
// 📤 Post Job to Target (Import) Instance
// 🚀 Main Migration Function

// 🔐========================= LOAD SERVICE KEYS =========================🔐
const exportServiceKey = JSON.parse(
  fs.readFileSync("export-service-key.txt", "utf8")
);

const importServiceKey = JSON.parse(
  fs.readFileSync("import-service-key.txt", "utf8")
);

// 🔑========================= AUTHENTICATION ============================🔑
// https://help.sap.com/docs/job-scheduling/sap-job-scheduling-service/authentication#service-plan-standard
async function getAccessToken(serviceKey) {
  const tokenUrl = `${serviceKey.uaa.url}/oauth/token`;
  const data = qs.stringify({
    grant_type: "client_credentials",
    client_id: serviceKey.uaa.clientid,
    client_secret: serviceKey.uaa.clientsecret,
  });

  const response = await axios.post(tokenUrl, data, {
    headers: {
      "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
    },
  });
  console.log(`🔑 Successfully fetched oAuth token from '${tokenUrl}'`);
  return response.data.access_token;
}

// 📥========================= RETRIEVE JOBS ================================📥
// https://help.sap.com/docs/job-scheduling/sap-job-scheduling-service/retrieve-jobs
async function getAllJobs(token) {
  const response = await axios.get(`${exportServiceKey.url}/scheduler/jobs?jobType=HTTP_ENDPOINT`, {
    headers: {
      Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`,
    },
  });

  return response.data;
}

// 🔍========================= RETRIEVE JOB DETAILS =========================🔍
// https://help.sap.com/docs/job-scheduling/sap-job-scheduling-service/retrieve-job-schedule-details
async function getJob(jobId, token) {
  const response = await axios.get(
    `${exportServiceKey.url}/scheduler/jobs/${jobId}?displaySchedules=true`,
    {
      headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` },
    }
  );
  return response.data;
}

// 🧹========================= CLEAN JOB DATA ============================🧹
function removeScheduleIdsFromJob(job) {
  // Remove fields that shouldn't be copied
  if (Array.isArray(job.schedules)) {
    job.schedules = job.schedules.map(({ scheduleId, ...rest }) => rest);
  }
  return job;
}

// 📤========================= CREATE JOB IN TARGET ========================📤
// https://help.sap.com/docs/job-scheduling/sap-job-scheduling-service/create-job
async function postJobToImportInstance(job, token, serviceKey) {
  console.log(`📦 Migrating job: '${job.name}' to instance: '${serviceKey.uaa.serviceInstanceId}'`);
  try {
    const response = await axios.post(
      `${serviceKey.url}/scheduler/jobs`,
      job,
      {
        headers: {
          Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`,
          "Content-Type": "application/json",
        },
      }
    );
    console.log(`✅ Job '${job.name}' imported successfully.`);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(
      `❌ Failed to import job '${job.name}':`,
      error.response?.data || error.message
    );
  }
}

// 🚀========================= MIGRATION PROCESS =========================🚀
async function migrateJobs() {
  const exportToken = await getAccessToken(exportServiceKey);
  const importToken = await getAccessToken(importServiceKey);

  const jobsResponse = await getAllJobs(exportToken);
  console.log(`⚙️ Number of jobs retrieved: '${jobsResponse.total}' from service instance ☁️ '${exportServiceKey.uaa.serviceInstanceId}'`);

  for (const job of jobsResponse.results) {
    const jobWithSchedules = await getJob(job.jobId, exportToken);
	// Let the system assign a new ID
	delete jobWithSchedules.jobId;
    const cleanedJobs = removeScheduleIdsFromJob(jobWithSchedules);
    
    await postJobToImportInstance(cleanedJobs, importToken, importServiceKey);
  }
}

// 🏁========================= START SCRIPT ==============================🏁
migrateJobs().catch(console.error);
 

🚀Running the Script

Note Ensure export-service-key.txt and import-service-key.txt are in the same directory as the script.

Install dependencies

npm install

Run the index.js

npm run start

You’ll see logs for each job being migrated, including success or error messages.

 

Terminal Output of Script Execution

✅Conclusion

Migrating jobs between SAP Job Scheduler instances doesn’t have to be a manual process. With a bit of scripting, you can automate the entire workflow and ensure consistency across environments.

This approach is especially helpful in CI/CD pipelines, when managing multiple BTP subaccounts, or when you want to migrate from one datacenter to another.

entry.title: SAP Digital Manufacturing Learning
entry.content:
Are you searching for content to kickstart your learning journey in SAP Digital Manufacturing?

In this blog post, I've compiled a comprehensive list of resources to help you familiarize yourself with the latest cloud-based MES offerings from SAP.

But first, what is SAP Digital Manufacturing?

SAP Digital Manufacturing is an enterprise-level, scalable, manufacturing solution that enables global manufacturers to manage and control manufacturing and shop floor operations and realize Industry 4.0 scenarios.

It’s a cloud-based Manufacturing Execution System (MES) that supports sustainable, risk-resilient manufacturing operations through a resource-efficient Industry 4.0 approach, enabling companies to integrate and embed intelligence in manufacturing processes.

It provides coordination of planning and execution processes, covering all aspects of the manufacturing cycle from planning to the shop floor and beyond.

Now, how can you learn it?

Discovering SAP Digital Manufacturing

This course is a comprehensive module aimed at providing an in-depth understanding of the placement, applicability, and integration possibilities of SAP Digital Manufacturing within the manufacturing vertical structure.

Analyzing Manufacturing Data in SAP Digital Manufacturing

This course aims at business users and consultants, who want to analyze the manufacturing data. The reporting options in SAP Digital Manufacturing are introduced which ensure traceability and generate insights to understand the root-causes of losses on the shop floor.

Configuring SAP Digital Manufacturing for Execution: Basic Data and Configuration

This enablement aims business users, consultants, solution architects, and industry specialists, who want to configure, execute, and control shop floor processes in the discrete manufacturing area using SAP Digital Manufacturing.

Executing Processes for Discrete Industries in SAP Digital Manufacturing

This course aims at business users and consultants, who want to execute shopfloor control processes in the discrete manufacturing area using SAP Digital Manufacturing.

Executing Processes for Process Industries in SAP Digital Manufacturing

This course is aimed at business users and consultants who want to execute shop floor control processes in the process manufacturing area using SAP Digital Manufacturing.

Implementing Machine Integration in SAP Digital Manufacturing

This course provides a comprehensive deep-dive into understanding and configuring the shop floor integration with SAP Digital Manufacturing to enable machine connectivity

Configuring Machine Integration in SAP Digital Manufacturing

This course provides the IT fundamentals of setting up and connecting the on-premise applications SAP Production Connector and SAP Cloud Connector required for machine connectivity with the cloud application SAP Digital Manufacturing.

Exploring Customization in SAP Digital Manufacturing

This course aims consultants, solution architects and industry specialists, who want to explore more about the extensibility mechanisms in SAP Digital Manufacturing. The course will especially focus on the Production Operator Dashboard Designer and the Production Process Designer.

You can also check SAP Digital Manufacturing IVJ Playlist below:

* Better viewed in Desktop or iPad in landscape mode

Hope it helps and inspires you!
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Joule based SAP HANA Application Migration Assistant - Transforming with Gen AI
entry.content:
In Early 2024, released first version of the SAP Business Application Studio extension which  Automates conversion of SAP HANA Database artifacts to SAP HANA Cloud artifacts.   SAP HANA  database objects (such as .hdbcds, .hdbtable, .hdbview, etc.) are transformed into their corresponding SAP CAP compliant database artifacts (e.g., .cds models).

The Q2-2025  release , enables service layer migration using GenAI capabilities in SAP Build Code. The assistant analyses SAP XS objects - xsodata ,xsjslib, xsjs and  files and generate corresponding service.cds, service.js and custom handler implementations under the CAP service layer. These AI capabilities  accelerates the migration of service layer while preserving structure and functional behaviour where possible. A sample conversion of xsodata to SAP CAP service definition is shown below. 

poWorklist.xsodata

service namespace "com.sap.openSAP.hana5.example.service"  { 
  "sap.hana.democontent.epm.models::PO_HEADER_EN" as "PO_WORKLIST" 
   keys ("PURCHASEORDERID","PURCHASEORDERITEM");
   
   "sap.hana.democontent.epm.models::PO_ITEM" as "PurchaseOrderItem" keys ("PURCHASEORDERID","PURCHASEORDERITEM");
   
   "PO.HeaderView" as "PurchaseOrderHeader" keys("PurchaseOrderId")
   navigates (     
        "PurchaseOrderHeader_PurchaseOrderItem" as "PurchaseOrderItems" from principal);

   association "PurchaseOrderHeader_PurchaseOrderItem"
      principal "PurchaseOrderHeader"("PurchaseOrderId") multiplicity "1"
      dependent "PurchaseOrderItem"("PurchaseOrderId") multiplicity "*";

}

service.cds

//converted from: /users/projects/hana-shine-xsa/core-xsjs/lib/xsodata/poWorklist.xsodata
service poWorklist @(path:'/poWorklist') {
    entity PO_WORKLIST as projection on SAP_HANA_DEMOCONTENT_EPM_MODELS_PO_HEADER_EN {
        *,
        PurchaseOrderItems: association to many PurchaseOrderItem on PurchaseOrderItems.PurchaseOrderId = $self.PURCHASEORDERID
    };

    entity PurchaseOrderItem as projection on SAP_HANA_DEMOCONTENT_EPM_MODELS_PO_ITEM;

    entity PurchaseOrderHeader as projection on PO.HeaderView {
        *,
        PurchaseOrderItems: association to many PurchaseOrderItem on PurchaseOrderItems.PurchaseOrderId = $self.PurchaseOrderId
    };
}

The converted code includes comments linking back to the original source files - making it easy for users to verify that key business logic has been accurately transformed.  In the similar way, all xsjslib and xsjs files are re-written as SAP CAP custom handlers. The resulting  modularised SAP CAP compliant project mirrors the original directory structure, ensuring familiarity and easy traceability.  All SAP HANA objects  will be adjusted automatically to follow SAP HANA Cloud naming conventions.

Additionally, the assistant generates a detailed report outlining recommended post-migration steps to guide users through final adjustments

As generative AI conversion is not guaranteed to be 100% accurate, human intervention is required post-migration to validate, refine, and productionize the application logic and service definitions.  

References

  • For prerequisites and step by step guide refer to corresponding xsc-to-cap  and xsa-to-cap repositories. 
  • Help Document: Migrating XS Classic Applications to SAP CAP
  • Help Document: SAP HANA XS Advanced Migration Guide

entry.title: Re: S4/HANA Cloud - How to block Customers and Supplier f...
entry.content:
Hello  @marceloromeiro 

The ODATA V2 API API_BUSINESS_PARTNER is the correct and supported option for this in Public Cloud. It provides access to the necessary blocking fields at both the Customer and Supplier levels, including

  • CompanyCodeData.BlockingIndicator (Customer)
  • SupplierCompany.BlockingIndicator or PurchasingOrg.PurchasingBlock (Supplier)

Reference

API_BUSINESS_PARTNER – SAP API Hub

No standalone Business Object provides that functionality directly in the Public Cloud.

With kind regard

Chuma

entry.title: Missing Documents numbers in LO delta extraction
entry.content:
We are using SAP standard extractors to extract the data from ECC system.  the following extractors are used to extract the SD numbers in BW

2LIS_11_V_SSL

2LIS_11_VAITM

2LIS_11_VAHDR

Daily delta extractions are happening successfully however we observed that on 2 instances few documents both created on changed on related to sales header, line item and schedule lines were missed in delta even though the bihourly delta extraction were successful on that day. Also, we noticed few billing documents which are extracted using 2lis_13 extractors are also missing for SD documents changed or created on the same days.

The deleted records were also missed in Delta as these are not updated in application table, so we are not able to recover the same.  Is there is any solution to recover the deleted records and update record deletion flag in BW. We have investigated on ECC end the LBWQ, SMQ1 and RSA7 and we could not find any documents stuck in the queue but found no failures.

Your immediate help is highly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Common issues in SAP Joule Activation in SAP S/4 HANA Private Cloud Edition
entry.content:
Hi @JithuVarghese ,
 

Are you using global user ID (UUID) in S/4 HANA system ? You can check so by going into SU01->Goto->External user id. If it's blank, then you're not using UUID in S/4 HANA system. In that case you can remove the UUID check in the condition from the target transformation. Only email check should be enough.

We understand the source transformation code is having some issues in help documentation. We will get it corrected. Here's the correct code:

 
{
  "user": {
    "mappings": [
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.USERNAME",
        "targetVariable": "entityIdSourceSystem"
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.USERNAME",
        "targetPath": "$.userName",
        "correlationAttribute": true
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.ALIAS.USERALIAS",
        "targetPath": "$.externalId",
        "optional": true,
        "correlationAttribute": true
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.SAPUSER_UUID.SAP_UID",
        "targetPath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User']['userUuid']",
        "optional": true
      },
      {
        "targetPath": "$.schemas[0]",
        "constant": "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User"
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.ADDRESS.E_MAIL",
        "targetPath": "$.emails[0].value",
        "optional": true,
        "correlationAttribute": true
      },
      {
        "targetPath": "$.emails[0].primary",
        "condition": "$.ADDRESS.E_MAIL EMPTY false",
        "constant": true
      },
      {
        "targetPath": "$.emails[0].type",
        "condition": "$.ADDRESS.E_MAIL EMPTY false",
        "constant": "work"
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.ADDRESS.FIRSTNAME",
        "targetPath": "$.name.givenName",
        "optional": true
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.ADDRESS.LASTNAME",
        "targetPath": "$.name.familyName"
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.ADDRESS.MIDDLENAME",
        "targetPath": "$.name.middleName",
        "optional": true
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.ADDRESS.NICKNAME",
        "targetPath": "$.nickName",
        "optional": true
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.ADDRESS.TITLE_P",
        "targetPath": "$.name.honorificPrefix",
        "optional": true
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.ADDRESS.COUNTRY",
        "targetPath": "$.addresses[0].country",
        "optional": true
      },
      {
        "targetPath": "$.addresses[0].primary",
        "condition": "$.ADDRESS.COUNTRY EMPTY false",
        "constant": true
      },
      {
        "targetPath": "$.addresses[0].type",
        "condition": "$.ADDRESS.COUNTRY EMPTY false",
        "constant": "work"
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.ADDRESS.TEL1_NUMBR",
        "targetPath": "$.phoneNumbers[0].value",
        "optional": true
      },
      {
        "targetPath": "$.phoneNumbers[0].primary",
        "condition": "$.ADDRESS.TEL1_NUMBR EMPTY false",
        "constant": true
      },
      {
        "targetPath": "$.phoneNumbers[0].type",
        "condition": "$.ADDRESS.TEL1_NUMBR EMPTY false",
        "constant": "work"
      },
      {
        "sourcePaths": ["$.DEFAULTS.LANGU"],
        "targetPath": "$.locale",
        "valueMappings": [
          {
            "key": ["W"],
            "mappedValue": "bg"
          }
        ],
        "optional": true,
        "type": "valueMapping",
        "defaultValue": "en"
      },
      {
        "sourcePaths": ["$.ADDRESS.LANGUP_ISO"],
        "targetPath": "$.preferredLanguage",
        "valueMappings": [
          {
            "key": ["W"],
            "mappedValue": "bg"
          }
        ],
        "optional": true,
        "type": "valueMapping",
        "defaultValue": "en",
        "functions": [
          {
            "function": "toLowerCaseString"
          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "sourcePaths": ["$.LOGONDATA.TZONE"],
        "targetPath": "$.timezone",
        "valueMappings": [
          {
            "key": ["EET"],
            "mappedValue": "Europe/Sofia"
          }
        ],
        "optional": true,
        "type": "valueMapping",
        "defaultValue": "Europe/Berlin"
      },
      {
        "targetPath": "$.active",
        "constant": false
      },
      {
        "targetPath": "$.active",
        "condition": "($.ISLOCKED.LOCAL_LOCK != 'L') && ($.ISLOCKED.GLOB_LOCK != 'L') && ($.ISLOCKED.WRNG_LOGON != 'L')",
        "constant": true
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.ACTIVITYGROUPS[*].AGR_NAME",
        "targetPath": "$.groups[?(@.value)]",
        "optional": true,
        "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true
      }
    ]
  },
  "group": {
    "mappings": [
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.ROLE_NAME",
        "targetVariable": "entityIdSourceSystem"
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.ROLE_NAME",
        "targetPath": "$.displayName",
        "functions": [
          {
            "function": "concatString",
            "condition": "'%abap.role.prefix%' !== 'null'",
            "prefix": "%abap.role.prefix%"
          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "targetPath": "$.schemas[0]",
        "constant": "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:Group"
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.USERLIST[*].USERNAME",
        "targetPath": "$.members[?(@.value)]",
        "optional": true,
        "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true
      }
    ]
  }
}

entry.title: Re: Common issues in SAP Joule Activation in SAP S/4 HANA Private Cloud Edition
entry.content:
Hi @S0027110563 ,

If you have followed all the steps and troubleshooting procedures and still the transactional queries are not getting resolved, kindly open an incident with CA-JOULE-PRV. 

You may check, in the monitor section in cloud connector, if the API call is coming through. If so, you can set up  trace in S/4 by using /IWFND/trace. If cloud connector log doesn't show anything, ticket is the only option for the team to check. 

 

Best regards, 

Samir

entry.title: SAP Cloud Access Controls cannot get transported: Object Type DRAS not permitted
entry.content:
Hi community,

I have created an access control (define accesspolicy) on SAP S/4 Public Cloud. It works perfectly but I am not able to release the the task to which the access control belongs.

I get the following error: "Object DRAS ZBUSINESSPARTNEROFUSER is not permitted. Object type DRAS cannot be transported."

ZBUSINESSPARTNEROFUSER looks like this:

define accesspolicy ZBUSINESSPARTNEROFUSER {
    @EndUserText.label: 'Business Partner of User'
    define aspect ZBUSINESSPARTNEROFUSER as
        select from ZI_BusinessUser with user element UserID
            {
                BusinessPartner //bypass when = 'bypass_value'
            }
            
}

This way I am able to define a role which checks for the business partner number of the logged on user:

@MappingRole: true
define role ZSOMEROLE {
    grant
        select
            on
                ZSOMECDSVIEW
                    where
                     CratedBy = $session.user or
                     (_SomeAssociation.BusinessPartnerId ) = aspect ZBUSINESSPARTNEROFUSER 
                     ;
}

 

Any help is very appreciated. I guess I will need to raise a SAP incident for this.

Thank you
Daniel

entry.title: Re: Add custom order by coumns in Story report
entry.content: Hi, I don't think it's possible, moreover, your User ID is not a number probably, so the sorting even there might not work correct. If you have time, you can try to explore some other options (not sure if it works 100%): Create several auxiliary calculated columns (for the User and a date with the timestamp) trying to do some ranking there, then add those columns to your table, sort them by ascending or descending and hide. Maybe this will work.

entry.title: ☕ Espresso Session: Customer Self-Service Portals and Container Shop for Waste Collection Companies
entry.content:
Join SAP and hmmh multimediahaus AG, one of Germany’s leading agencies for connected commerce, for a dynamic Espresso Session focused on digital transformation in the utilities and waste & recycling sectors.

For over 25 years, hmmh has been at the forefront of innovation, helping national and international companies build seamless digital experiences. With a team of 300 digital specialists across six global locations, hmmh delivers robust e-commerce platforms and customer self-service portals powered by the SAP Customer Experience (CX) portfolio.

 

What to Expect

In just 30 minutes, you’ll gain valuable insights into:

  • Architectural Overview of customer portal solutions
  • Key Features & Functionalities tailored for waste collection companies
  • User Experience Best Practices for self-service portals

We’ll showcase successful implementations for:

  • ALBA Group – a leading environmental services provider
  • Wirtschaftsbetriebe Duisburg – municipal utility and waste management

 

Why Attend?

Whether you're in IT, operations, or customer service, this session will help you understand how SAP Commerce Cloud can:

  • Enhance customer engagement
  • Streamline container ordering and service requests
  • Reduce operational overhead through automation

 

Ready to Join?

📅 Date: Tuesday, 08 July 2025
🕓 Time: 16:00 - 16:30 CEST

Don’t miss this opportunity to explore how digital portals can revolutionize customer service in the waste management industry and register at: Customer Self-Service Portals and Container Shop for Waste Collection companies – Powered by SAP Commerce Cloud

entry.title: Re: Crystal Report Runtime Unused Formula Performance
entry.content:
Hi Patrick,

That is normal behaviour... 

Go here to the latest SP:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

On that page is a sample app I wrote to check the reports formulae, it's in the drop down list:

how-to-parameters-in-crystal-reports-for-visual-studio-net

If they are not used then delete them as you noted.

The reason why is a result of CR Server/BOE products. When publishing a report to the CMS those products assume the reports are 100% complete. Unlike CR Designer which is a design tool, it allows you to load bad formula and other things simply because it's a designer and assumes everything is good but it doesn't load everything and verify them.

The SDK's do load all report objects into the various collections and does some Verify in the back end, because it assumes all objects are used as noted above.

You could modify the above test app to load all reports and check the formulae, delete all unused and save them to a new folder so you don't modify the original, just in case...

Don

entry.title: Re: Output tables in Kmeans
entry.content:
Thanks for your help @Xuanqi_Liu, I share the code of the sample dataset I'm using. The input table for KMeans is the encoded table: 

 
CREATE COLUMN TABLE DATA_INPUT_CLIENTI_ENERGIA (
"ID" INTEGER,
"NOME" NVARCHAR (50),
"RANGE_ETA" NVARCHAR (10),
"REGIONE" NVARCHAR (30),
"AREA_GEOGRAFICA" NVARCHAR (20),
"CONSUMO_KWH_TOTALE" DECIMAL (6,2),
"CONSUMO_KWH_F1" DECIMAL (6,2),
"CONSUMO_KWH_F2" DECIMAL (6,2), 
"CONSUMO_KWH_F3" DECIMAL (6,2),
"COSTO_KWH" DECIMAL (4,3),
"TIPO_USO" NVARCHAR (20)
);
 
DO BEGIN
    DECLARE i INT = 1;
    DECLARE totale DECIMAL(6,2);
    DECLARE f1 DECIMAL(6,2);
    DECLARE f2 DECIMAL(6,2);
    DECLARE f3 DECIMAL(6,2);
    DECLARE max_f2_perc DECIMAL(5,4);
    DECLARE f1_perc DECIMAL(5,4);
    DECLARE f2_perc DECIMAL(5,4);
    DECLARE regione VARCHAR(50);
    DECLARE area_geografica VARCHAR(20);
 
    WHILE :i <= 10000 DO
        totale := ROUND(500 + RAND() * 4500, 2);  -- Totale tra 500 e 5000
 
        f1_perc := 0.30 + RAND() * 0.20;
        f1 := ROUND(totale * f1_perc, 2);
 
        max_f2_perc := LEAST(0.50, 1.00 - f1_perc);  -- max 50%, ma non oltre 100%-F1
        f2_perc := 0.30 + RAND() * (max_f2_perc - 0.30);
        f2 := ROUND(totale * f2_perc, 2);
 
        f3 := ROUND(totale - f1 - f2, 2);
        
        regione := CASE MOD(:i, 20)
            WHEN 0 THEN 'Lombardia'
            WHEN 1 THEN 'Lazio'
            WHEN 2 THEN 'Campania'
            WHEN 3 THEN 'Sicilia'
            WHEN 4 THEN 'Veneto'
            WHEN 5 THEN 'Emilia-Romagna'
            WHEN 6 THEN 'Piemonte'
            WHEN 7 THEN 'Puglia'
            WHEN 8 THEN 'Toscana'
            WHEN 9 THEN 'Calabria'
            WHEN 10 THEN 'Sardegna'
            WHEN 11 THEN 'Liguria'
            WHEN 12 THEN 'Marche'
            WHEN 13 THEN 'Abruzzo'
            WHEN 14 THEN 'Friuli-Venezia Giulia'
            WHEN 15 THEN 'Trentino-Alto Adige'
            WHEN 16 THEN 'Umbria'
            WHEN 17 THEN 'Basilicata'
            WHEN 18 THEN 'Molise'
            ELSE 'Valle d Aosta'
        END;
 
        area_geografica := CASE
            WHEN regione IN ('Piemonte', 'Lombardia', 'Veneto', 'Emilia-Romagna', 'Liguria', 'Friuli-Venezia Giulia', 
            'Trentino-Alto Adige', 'Valle d Aosta') THEN 'Nord'
            WHEN regione IN ('Toscana', 'Marche', 'Lazio', 'Umbria', 'Abruzzo') THEN 'Centro'
            WHEN regione IN ('Campania', 'Puglia', 'Molise', 'Basilicata', 'Calabria') THEN 'Sud'
            WHEN regione IN ('Sicilia', 'Sardegna') THEN 'Isole'
            ELSE 'Altro'
        END;
 
        INSERT INTO DATA_INPUT_CLIENTI_ENERGIA (
            ID, 
            NOME, 
            RANGE_ETA, 
            REGIONE, 
            AREA_GEOGRAFICA,
            CONSUMO_KWH_TOTALE, 
            CONSUMO_KWH_F1, 
            CONSUMO_KWH_F2, 
            CONSUMO_KWH_F3,
            COSTO_KWH, 
            TIPO_USO
        )
        VALUES (
            :i,
            'cliente_' || :i,
            CASE MOD(:i, 5)
                WHEN 0 THEN '18-25'
                WHEN 1 THEN '26-35'
                WHEN 2 THEN '36-45'
                WHEN 3 THEN '46-60'
                ELSE '60+'
            END,
            :regione,
            :area_geografica,
            :totale,
            :f1,
            :f2,
            :f3,
            ROUND(0.150 + RAND() * 0.150, 3),
            CASE MOD(:i, 4)
                WHEN 0 THEN 'Residenziale'
                WHEN 1 THEN 'Industriale'
                WHEN 2 THEN 'Commerciale'
                ELSE 'Agricolo'
            END
        );
        i := :i + 1;
    END WHILE;
END;
 
CREATE COLUMN TABLE DATA_INPUT_ENCODED_CLIENTI_ENERGIA (
"ID" INTEGER,
"NOME" NVARCHAR (50),
"RANGE_ETA_ENCODED" INTEGER,
"REGIONE_ENCODED" INTEGER,
"AREA_GEOGRAFICA_ENCODED" INTEGER,
"CONSUMO_KWH_TOTALE" DECIMAL (6,2),
"CONSUMO_KWH_F1" DECIMAL (6,2),
"CONSUMO_KWH_F2" DECIMAL (6,2),
"CONSUMO_KWH_F3" DECIMAL (6,2),
"COSTO_KWH" DECIMAL (4,3),
"TIPO_USO_ENCODED" INTEGER
);
 
INSERT INTO DATA_INPUT_ENCODED_CLIENTI_ENERGIA
SELECT 
ID,
nome,
CASE
WHEN range_eta = '18-25' THEN 0
WHEN range_eta = '26-35' THEN 1
WHEN range_eta = '36-45' THEN 2
WHEN range_eta = '46-60' THEN 3
WHEN range_eta = '60+' THEN 4
ELSE 5
END AS range_eta_encoded,
CASE
WHEN regione = 'Lombardia' THEN 0
WHEN regione = 'Lazio' THEN 1
WHEN regione = 'Campania' THEN 2
WHEN regione = 'Sicilia' THEN 3
WHEN regione = 'Veneto' THEN 4
WHEN regione = 'Emilia-Romagna' THEN 5
WHEN regione = 'Piemonte' THEN 6
WHEN regione = 'Puglia' THEN 7
WHEN regione = 'Toscana' THEN 8
WHEN regione = 'Calabria' THEN 9
WHEN regione = 'Sardegna' THEN 10
WHEN regione = 'Liguria' THEN 11
WHEN regione = 'Marche' THEN 12
WHEN regione = 'Abruzzo' THEN 13
WHEN regione = 'Friuli-Venezia Giulia' THEN 14
WHEN regione = 'Trentino-Alto Adige' THEN 15
WHEN regione = 'Umbria' THEN 16
WHEN regione = 'Basilicata' THEN 17
WHEN regione = 'Molise' THEN 18
ELSE 19
END AS regione_encoded,
CASE
WHEN area_geografica = 'Nord' THEN 0
WHEN area_geografica = 'Centro' THEN 1
WHEN area_geografica = 'Sud' THEN 2
WHEN area_geografica = 'Isole' THEN 4
ELSE 5
END AS area_geografica_encoded,
CONSUMO_KWH_TOTALE,
CONSUMO_KWH_F1,
CONSUMO_KWH_F2,
CONSUMO_KWH_F3,
COSTO_KWH,
CASE
WHEN tipo_uso = 'Residenziale' THEN 0
WHEN tipo_uso = 'Industriale' THEN 1
WHEN tipo_uso = 'Agricolo' THEN 2
WHEN tipo_uso = 'Commerciale' THEN 3
ELSE 4
END AS tipo_uso_encoded
FROM DATA_INPUT_CLIENTI_ENERGIA;

I look forward for your reply, thank you

entry.title: Re: Time Management-Impact of Editing the field present i...

Hi @Ashwal_ 

In the MDF object, when a field is in "Not Visible", it won't be visible to anyone. When it is changed to "Editable or Read-Only" then it will be visible in the Time Off UI for all users and to control the field visibility; you need to use Manage Permission Role to make it's visibility by RBP.

Thanks

Arun

entry.title: Re: Fiori over mobile
entry.content:
Recommended SAP Fiori client application (iOS/Android)

If you mean the former "SAP Fiori client" app it was removed from app stores in the end of Q1/2022.

2992772 - Removal of SAP Fiori Client From Public App Stores - SAP for Me 

Currently the alternative is the SAP Mobile Start app

SAP Mobile Start on the App Store

SAP Mobile Start - Apps on Google Play

There is no special configurations that you need on Front end server. Of course, you should have one of the 2 approaches below:

1) Expose Front end system on the internet so it's easily reachable from mobile devices on any public network. This implies the usage of web dispatcher and firewalls.
2) Do not expose the front end system and use VPN on mobile devices

There is no mobile-specific roles or authorizations. However bear in mind there is a "device type" configuration on target mappings which controls if a given app is visible in desktops, tablets or smartphones. But this has nothing to do with being able to access the launchpad from a mobile device.

entry.title: Custom fields not updating BAPI_PO_CHANGE extensionin
entry.content:
I am currently working on a project where I need to update custom fields in a purchase order using the BAPI_PO_CHANGE function module. Despite my efforts, the custom fields zorig_qty  and zorig_meins  in the EKPO table are not updating as expected. Below is the code I am using:

DATA: lt_return TYPE TABLE OF bapiret2 .
DATA: lt_poitem TYPE TABLE OF bapimepoitem.
DATA: lt_poitemx TYPE TABLE OF bapimepoitemx.
DATA: ls_bapi_poitem  TYPE bapi_te_mepoitem.
DATA: ls_bapi_poitemx TYPE bapi_te_mepoitemx.
DATA: lt_extensionin TYPE TABLE OF bapiparex .
DATA: ls_extensionin TYPE bapiparex.
DATA: ls_return LIKE LINE OF lt_return .
DATA: ls_poitem LIKE LINE OF lt_poitem.
DATA: ls_poitemx LIKE LINE OF lt_poitemx.
DATA: lv_ebeln  TYPE ebeln.
DATA: lv_qty TYPE zorig_qty.
DATA lv_char_zorig_qty TYPE c LENGTH 13.
DATA lv_char_zorig_meins TYPE c LENGTH 3.

CLEAR: ls_return, ls_poitem, ls_poitemx, ls_extensionin, lt_extensionin, ls_bapi_poitem, ls_bapi_poitemx.

lv_ebeln = '4500165660'.
lv_qty = 150.

*** zvelden updaten in ekpo
ls_bapi_poitem-po_item = '00010'.
ls_bapi_poitem-zorig_qty = lv_qty.
ls_bapi_poitem-zorig_meins = 'KG'.


ls_bapi_poitemx-po_item = '00010'.
ls_bapi_poitemx-zorig_qty  = 'X'.
ls_bapi_poitemx-zorig_meins = 'X'.

ls_extensionin-structure = 'BAPI_TE_MEPOITEM'.
MOVE ls_bapi_poitem-po_item TO ls_extensionin-valuepart1(5).
MOVE ls_bapi_poitem-zorig_qty TO lv_char_zorig_qty.
MOVE lv_char_zorig_qty TO ls_extensionin-valuepart1+22(16).
MOVE ls_bapi_poitem-zorig_meins TO lv_char_zorig_meins.
MOVE lv_char_zorig_meins TO ls_extensionin-valuepart1+39(3).
APPEND ls_extensionin TO lt_extensionin.

CLEAR ls_extensionin.
ls_extensionin-structure = 'BAPI_TE_MEPOITEMX'.
ls_extensionin-valuepart1 = ls_bapi_poitemx.
APPEND ls_extensionin TO lt_extensionin.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_PO_CHANGE'
  EXPORTING
    purchaseorder = lv_ebeln
  TABLES
    return        = lt_return
    extensionin   = lt_extensionin
    extensionout  = lt_extensionin.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
  EXPORTING
    wait = abap_true.

I'm getting a return message of Standard PO 4500165660 changed but when I checked the table it is not changed

entry.title: SAP FIORI | Timestamp conversion in fiori front end
entry.content:
Hi SAP  Experts,

I've created a timestamp column in the backend by concatenating the creation date (VBAK-ERDAT) and creation time (VBAK-ERZET). The resulting timestamp is in the format YYYYMMDDHHMMSS, and the values are being generated as expected.

Example:

  • Creation Date: April 25, 2025 (20250425)
  • Creation Time: 5:14:06 AM (051406)
  • Timestamp (backend): 20250425051406

    However, after exposing the CDS view through a Fiori application, the timestamp displayed on the frontend appears to have an additional ~5.5 hours added to it.

    Frontend Example:

    • Timestamp displayed: April 25, 2025, 10:44:06 AM (20250425104406)

      It seems that the SAP Gateway or the Fiori frontend is performing a timezone conversion on the timestamp value.

      As per SAP Note 3215029, this behavior aligns with the standard design. The timestamp shown in UI5 applications may differ from backend values depending on the browser's timezone settings.

      Has anyone else encountered a similar issue? Any suggestions or best practices to handle this discrepancy would be greatly appreciated.

      Best regards,

      Mukhtar

entry.title: Re: Want to configure SAP Cloud ALM for SAP Cell and Gene...
entry.content:
According to the SAP Cloud ALM Supported Solutions guide, SAP Cell and Gene Therapy Orchestration is currently supported by SAP Cloud ALM for 2 Operations use cases and no Implementation use cases.

  • Integration & Exception Monitoring
  • Real User Monitoring

The SAP Cloud ALM for Operations Expert Portal Setup Managed Components page has a guide for configuring SAP Cell and Gene Therapy Orchestration for integration with SAP Cloud ALM.

If you need to check these topics in the future for other products, please refer to these KBAs:

  • 3386833 - Where to find a list of SAP Cloud ALM supported solutions in Implementation and Operations?
  • 3426195 - What SAP Cloud ALM monitoring and metrics are available for my SAP product?

entry.title: How to manage formal name in SuccessFactors?
entry.content:
In Onboarding, my understanding is that formal full name is automatically updated by combining first and last name. However, practically, combining the first and last name does not always make the names used in official documents. 

Below two are the possible solution that I came up with.

1) Configuring the item in People Profile after the candidate starts working.

2) Using other field such as Preferred Name instead of Formal Name.

I would love to hear the use cases on how to manage official names. Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Hiring Agents to reinforce your P2P team with SAP: When smart AI Agents become your new colleagues
entry.content:
Can you imagine an augmented team, working like a dream team, to help you negotiate, evaluate, and award an RFQ with your Vendors?  We're actively experimenting with this approach to bring the vision to reality for our clients.

Let’s start simple

We assume that today Personas are primarily working in a traditional manner, performing repetitive and time-consuming manual tasks across the various steps of the Procure-to-Pay (P2P) business process.

As an example, we can represent a portion of the P2P process like this:

People collaborate and communicate across departments and with their partner ecosystems, interacting with both SAP and non-SAP backend systems to gather the inputs needed to proceed with their tasks. These interactions are dynamic and iterative, driven by continuously evolving daily business needs.

Let’s assume we can now move a few boxes - representing the current 'as-is' process steps - from being 100% manual to something more advanced. This could involve elements from previous RPA investments, innovations from the new paradigm of Generative AI, or other emerging solutions. We can represent it like this:

What are we aiming for?

From RFQ creation to awarding and document generation, we envision a flow where different technologies can help in achieving:

  • Smart RFQ Creation: where e.g. SAP Joule Agent will suggest the most suitable Vendors. No panic, humans will make the final call.
  • Automated Response Processing: RPA e.g. from UiPath, and document recognition will handle heavy lifting.
  • Collaborative Evaluation: Agents based on different technologies (e.g., MyWave, Azure, others), and operating across departments such as Procurement, Production, and Finance, will analyze pricing and conditions, and evaluate related risks - working together in an Agent-to-Agent concerto.
  • Awarding Proposal: Gen-AI will suggest optimal awarding scenarios. You’ll validate them.
  • Document Automation: From award letters to purchase orders, everything will flow seamlessly.

Breaking silos, building a modern collaboration

With this flexible and scalable hyper-automated approach, we aim to break down traditional departmental silos. Information will flow seamlessly, decisions will be more informed, and response times will be drastically reduced. No worries about if colleagues are enjoying the beach during the summer or they are too busy to provide input - your needs will still be met.

Elevate Humans to focus on higher-value tasks

We are experimenting with Agents, autonomous or semi-autonomous, as new colleagues trained in the procurement business segment - such as in the automotive industry. These Agents support humans by using this solution to detect risks (e.g., identifying suppliers in conflict zones or with questionable labor practices), compare prices against market trends, and ensure production is not delayed due to logistics issues.

So, are you ready to hire your future digital colleagues?
I know you might be from the Order-to-Cash business process, right? This is a representation of the Procure-to-Pay process, but we're scaling across all business processes 😉

#EnjoyAI

entry.title: Re: I Killed Fiori (And I'd Do It Again)
entry.content:
Hi @junwu 👋,

Tagged it fiori(ui) because that's where the pain lives—anyone in that tag is debugging why tiles won't load or decoding cryptic @ui annotations.

Yes, this logic could kill anything. I could replace email with smoke signals, but that misses the point. 😏

TRIZ principle: If something exists only to justify its own existence, eliminate it.

Fiori screens that wrap a single OData call in eight clicks aren't "user experience"—they're CSS cosplay. So:

  • Keep: Rich dashboards, complex approvals, anything adding genuine insight
  • Ax: CRUD pageants that turn API calls into click marathons

Some UIs earn their keep. Most are just expensive theater.

Cheers!

entry.title: Re: Business Objects with Microsoft Fabric
entry.content:
Hi Brian,

Not clear how you configured the reports in BOE, you should be able to set default log on info for each report.

Is viewing the reports for all users failing, if so likely issue is the DB client is not configure for BOE to access, when you say ODBC is configure are you saying it's working on the BOE Server or only in the Report Designer?

Crystal reports fully supports DB security, for those who can't see the reports when you will need to do is either schedule the reports and allow those user who don't have access to view them or set the log on info as an administrator in Report and again set the log on info in BOE database setup etc.

There are other ways to allow them but you need to decide who can access the reports using various credentials etc.

Note - using Admin rights may give them access to modify the report SQL to get access to all data...

Don

entry.title: Re: Unable to mass update product master data
entry.content:
• Missing Authorizations:

    • Ensure your user role has the necessary authorizations for mass changes. Check for roles including access to:

      • MM_PUR_MAINTAIN or similar authorization objects.

      • OData service /SAP/PRODUCT_MASS_PROC_SRV (depending on your system version).

  • Product Selection Criteria:

    • Verify that all selected products are valid and belong to the same product type (e.g., material type) or organization level.

    • Some inconsistencies (e.g., blocked materials or different product categories) can cause mass processing to fail.

entry.title: Re: Covert Date format from 00000 to Null value.
entry.content:
Hi,

Don't use separate fields for date and time in OData services, but use timestamps instead. Separate fields lead to multiple issues, like that the initial time (000000) is also a valid time (00:00:00) and gets ugly with timezone handling.

If you construct a timestamp out of initial GATE_DATE_OUT and GATE_TIME_OUT, then it will be shown as blank.

BR,
Alex

entry.title: Re: Workflow Process & Enhancement on Routine maintenance...
entry.content:
1. Define User Status Profile (BS02)

  • Transaction Code: BS02

  • New Profile Name: ZPM_NOTIF_APPR

Status Definitions:

  • PEND - Pending Approval (Initial Status)

  • APPR - Approved

  • REJ - Rejected

Business Transaction Control:

  • PEND Status:

    • Set as initial status

    • Forbid NOCO (Notification Completion)

    • Optionally restrict ORCR (Order Creation) and task processing

  • APPR Status:

    • Permit all follow-up processing

  • REJ Status:

    • Forbid all follow-up processing

✅ Save and activate the status profile

2. Assign Status Profile to Notification Type

Path: SPRO → Plant Maintenance → Notifications → Define Notification Types

  1. Select notification type (e.g., M1 - Breakdown Notification)

  2. Assign status profile: ZPM_NOTIF_APPR

✅ Save configuration

3. Design Workflow Template (PFTC_INS)

  • Object Type: BUS2038 (PM Notification)

  • Triggering Event: CREATED

Workflow Structure:

  1. Decision Step: "Approve Maintenance Notification"

    • Recipients: Assigned HOD/Approver

    • Decision Options: Approve/Reject

  2. Outcome Branch:

    • Approve: Set status to APPR using FM STATUS_CHANGE_EXTERN

    • Reject:

      • Set status to REJ

      • Send message to creator via Send Mail step

✅ Activate and test template

4. Link Workflow to Notification Event (SWETYPV)

  • Object Type: BUS2038

  • Event: CREATED

  • Receiver Type: Your workflow template

✅ Activate event linkage

5. Define Agent Assignment

Option 1: Organizational Structure (PPOMW)

  1. Create/maintain organizational units

  2. Assign users to responsible positions

Option 2: Rule-Based Determination (PFAC)

  • Create rules based on:

    • Work Center

    • Planner Group

    • Functional Location

✅ Configure for dynamic approver assignment

6. Notification Configuration (Optional)

  1. Configure SAPConnect (SCOT) for emails

  2. Ensure user email addresses in SU01

  3. Add Send Mail steps in workflow for:

    • Rejection notifications with reason

    • Approval confirmations

entry.title: Re: SAP FIORI | Timestamp conversion in fiori front end
entry.content:
Thanks for your reply. I have tried with converting the timestamp to UTC using dats_tims_to_tstmp() but the front end is converting the timestamps.

 

       cast( dats_tims_to_tstmp( _SalesDoc.CreationDate,_SalesDoc. CreationTime, 'UTC', $session.client, 'NULL' )   as timestamp )          as CreatedAt,

entry.title: Re: SAP FIORI | Timestamp conversion in fiori front end
entry.content: Yes, this is the expected behavior, that the FE converts the timestamp. Is you timestamp really in UTC? What did you pass as the tzone parameter to dats_tims_to_tstmp()? It should likely be abap_system_timezone( $session.client,'NULL' ).

entry.title: Re: SAP FIORI | Timestamp conversion in fiori front end
entry.content: The line should be: cast( dats_tims_to_tstmp( _SalesDoc.CreationDate, _SalesDoc.CreationTime, abap_system_timezone( $session.client,'NULL' ), $session.client, 'NULL' ) as timestamp ) as CreatedAt,

entry.title: Re: Migration story of 48 SAP SuccessFactors HCM successful integrations in 4 Months
entry.content:
Hi, @KavyaManohara  thank for your share. An typicial example for integration best practices.

What particularly impresses me is your comprehensive utilization of SAP SuccessFactors Integration Center (IC) templates for all integrations ranging from simple to moderately complex scenarios.​​

In most customer environments, there is a strong demand for integration architecture standardization and simplification—typically achieved through API-based integration as the unified approach.

​That said, I believe the Integration Center represents an often-underestimated yet highly valuable tool.​​ It offers several key advantages:

  • ​Cost-effectiveness
  • ​No-code implementation​
  • ​Full control by SF consultants​
  • ​Flexible adaptability within defined scope​

These characteristics make IC particularly suitable for scenarios where rapid deployment and minimal technical overhead are priorities.

entry.title: Are there any substitutions for WorkZone and S4HANA Cloud when using the BTP Consumption Monitor?
entry.content:
Hi everyone, I recently successfully created/implemented the BTP Consumption Monitor as shown by Willem Pardaens in the YouTube video linked to the Discovery Mission. I personally used WorkZone advanced edition and S4HANA Cloud for it. However, I have a bit of a 2-part question.

1. Is it possible to integrate with or use the Fiori Launchpad service as the front end instead of WorkZone (standard or advanced) since a lot of potential clients already use the Fiori Launchpad service?

2. Are there any substitutes for S4HANA Cloud? (my subaccount was in a region where the S4HANA Cloud free trial was not available so I used the paid version. While that wasn't necessarily a big problem for me, potential clients might also be in a region where the free HANA Cloud trial is also not available and want to use something else)
